---
author: "James Hetherington"
date: 2026-06-04
format: revealjs
title: "A talk about UCL's model for Research IT"
---

# Science and Engineering

{{< image-credit
src="https://research.jh.arc.ucl.ac.uk/inaugural/assets/generated/science_and_engineering.svg"
alt="Diagram showing the relationship between science and engineering"
width="100%" >}}

# A Definition

**Engineering Computational Science is the development and application of tools, practices and systems for the efficient,
trustworthy, ethical and safe use of information technologies as part of trying to produce knowledge about the world.**

# Epistemic Cultures

{{< image-credit
src="https://research.jh.arc.ucl.ac.uk/inaugural/assets/stored/epistemologies.png"
width="80%"
alt="A cartoon illustrating different cultures of knowledge production, from humanities to astronomy."
credit="Generated with the DALL·E image model, OpenAI, accessed via ChatGPT (April 2026)"
credit-url="https://chatgpt.com/share/69f1e5c2-27b0-8387-b2a7-aed7b0d5483d" >}}

# Service provision as an academic practice

:::: {.two-col}
::: {}
{{< image-credit
src="https://research.jh.arc.ucl.ac.uk/inaugural/assets/generated/arc.svg"
alt="UCL ARC's hybrid academic-service model"
width="100%" >}}
:::
::: {}
* Utility
* Curiosity
* Community
:::
::::

# Has it worked?

:::: {.two-col}
::: {}
* Grown from ~50 to ~150, mostly from external funding
* [NCR Charger](https://www.ucl.ac.uk/news/2026/feb/ucl-host-ps195m-supercomputing-facility)
* [CCMI CDT](https://ccmi-cdt.org)
* FRIDGE TRE work mentioned in UK AI for Science strategy
:::
::: {}

* Constant back and forth over identity
* Resource contention between services and grants is hard
* Service transfer with IT is still bumpy (but improving) 
:::
::::
